prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response: Here's one possibility: you may be overestimating how much other people pay attention to and judge you for minor mistakes or imperfections. Many people assume they are being scrutinized much more than they actually are, when in reality most people are primarily focused on themselves. This "spotlight effect" can lead to unnecessary self-consciousness and anxiety. Of course, I don't know you personally so this is just a general observation that applies to many people. Let me know if you'd like me to pick something else!
